activities.description: Chat, Slack, Discord, and text messaging
activities.instructions: Levels: high=active conversation, medium=reading messages, low=chat app visible but idle. Detect via: chat app UI, message notifications, typing indicators.
activities.name: Messaging

activities.instructions: Levels: high=composing or actively reading email, medium=scanning inbox, low=email client visible but idle. Detect via: email client UI, inbox view, compose window.
activities.name: Email

activities.instructions: Levels: high=actively speaking/presenting, medium=listening attentively, low=muted or multitasking during call. Detect via: video call UI, multiple speakers, calendar event visible.
activities.name: Meetings

defaults.description: Chat, Slack, Discord, and text messaging
defaults.instructions: Levels: high=active conversation, medium=reading messages, low=chat app visible but idle. Detect via: chat app UI, message notifications, typing indicators.
defaults.name: Messaging

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=composing or actively reading email, medium=scanning inbox, low=email client visible but idle. Detect via: email client UI, inbox view, compose window.
defaults.name: Email

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=running commands or scripts, medium=reading output, low=terminal open but idle. Detect via: shell prompts, command output, tmux/screen sessions. If terminal use is clearly coding-related, prefer coding instead.
defaults.name: Terminal

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=actively creating or editing, medium=reviewing designs, low=design tool open but idle. Detect via: design apps (Figma, Photoshop, etc), canvas editing.
defaults.name: Design

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=actively composing text, medium=editing/revising, low=document open but not being edited. Detect via: document editors, note apps, text content changing.
defaults.name: Writing

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=actively playing, medium=in menus or waiting, low=game open but tabbed out. Detect via: game window, controller input, game UI elements.
defaults.name: Gaming

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=actively choosing or browsing music, medium=playlist running while working, low=ambient background audio. Detect via: music player UI, audio playback indicators.
defaults.name: Music

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=focused reading, medium=skimming content, low=document open but attention elsewhere. Detect via: PDF viewers, article pages, documentation sites. Do not use for reading code — that is coding.
defaults.name: Reading

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=writing or debugging code, medium=reading/reviewing code, low=IDE or editor open but not focused. Detect via: editors, terminals with dev tools, AI coding assistants, git operations. Includes focused code reading and thinking.
defaults.name: Coding

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=posting or actively engaging, medium=scrolling feed, low=social app open but idle. Detect via: social media sites/apps, feed content, post composition.
defaults.name: Social Media

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=actively editing or organizing, medium=reviewing data, low=app open but not focused. Detect via: spreadsheet/slide editors, project management tools, calendar apps, task boards.
defaults.name: Productivity

defaults.description: Video calls, in-person meetings, and conferences
defaults.instructions: Levels: high=actively speaking/presenting, medium=listening attentively, low=muted or multitasking during call. Detect via: video call UI, multiple speakers, calendar event visible.
defaults.name: Meetings

defaults.instructions: Levels: high=actively watching, medium=video playing while doing something else, low=video paused or minimized. Detect via: video player UI, streaming sites, playback controls.
defaults.name: Video

defaults.description: Web browsing, research, and reading online
defaults.instructions: Levels: high=actively navigating/researching, medium=reading a page, low=browser open but idle. Detect via: browser tabs, URL changes, search queries.
defaults.name: Browsing
